basic_details.job_title: Tripura High Court Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 17 Junior Administrative Assistant and Senior Computer Assistant Posts
basic_details.total_vacancies: Junior Administrative Assistant, Senior Computer Assistant
basic_details.short_information: Tripura High Court Recruitment 2026 has been officially announced through two separate notifications dated 7th July 2026, inviting online applications for a total of 17 vacancies - 14 posts of Junior Administrative Assistant (Advertisement No. HC-02/2026) and 3 posts of Senior Computer Assistant (Advertisement No. HC-03/2026). Online applications for both posts open on 10.07.2026 at 11:00 A.M. and close on 10.08.2026 at 5:00 P.M. This article covers eligibility, age limit, pay scale, application fee, selection process, and the step-by-step application procedure for both posts based on the offi

application_fee.details: Fees must be paid online through Credit Card (Visa/Master/RuPay), Net Banking, Debit Card (Visa/Master/RuPay), Wallets, Cash Cards, or UPI only. No other mode of payment is accepted, and bank charges may apply. Fees once deposited will not be refunded or adjusted against any other recruitment in future. ST/SC candidates belonging to other states must apply as general category candidates and pay the fee applicable to the general category.

age_limit.details: As on 10.08.2026, candidates must be: | Not below 18 years of age Not more than 40 years of age for candidates of other (general) categories Not more than 45 years of age for candidates belonging to Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes | This age limit applies uniformly to both the Junior Administrative Assistant and Senior Computer Assistant posts.

qualification.details: A person is not eligible for appointment to the service if he/she: | Is not a citizen of India Has been dismissed from service by the Government, Central Government, High Court, or any statutory/local authority Has been convicted of an offence involving moral turpitude Has more than one spouse living Directly or indirectly attempts to influence the recruiting authority by any means

salary_details.junior administrative assistant: Entry pay of Rs. 21,400 per month in Pay Matrix Level-7 of TSCS (Revised Pay) (First Amendment) Rules, 2018, subject to revision by the Government from time to time
salary_details.senior computer assistant: Regular scale Pay Band-2, Rs. 5,700-24,000 with Grade Pay of Rs. 2,800

selection_process: Written Test (English) - Comprehension, Précis Writing, Paragraph Writing, Letter Writing, Translation (Bengali-English & vice versa), Writing Ability 30
Skill Test (Practical demonstration: knowledge of software and hardware) 25
etc. Minimum qualifying marks: 60% (relaxable by 10% for reserved category candidates). Shortlisting ratio for Phase 2: 1:3 (i.e.
Knowledge of Computer Science and allied matters
if no suitable reserved-category candidate is available
the High Court will first attempt to fill the post from candidates of other reserved categories; if still unavailable
the post will be filled as per applicable rules by candidates outside the ST/SC category.
how_to_apply: Visit the official website of the High Court of Tripura at thc.nic.in.
Click on the "Recruitment" tab on the homepage.
Select the relevant advertisement - HC-02/2026 for Junior Administrative Assistant or HC-03/2026 for Senior Computer Assistant.
Read the instructions carefully in the "How to Apply" segment of the Online Application Portal before proceeding.
Register and fill in the online application form with accurate personal, educational, and category details.
Upload the required documents/photograph/signature as specified in the portal.
Pay the applicable application fee online through Credit Card, Debit Card, Net Banking, UPI, or Wallet.
Submit the form and save/download the confirmation page and Registration Number for future reference.
The High Court will not entertain hard copy applications; submissions must be made only through the Online Application Portal.
important_instructions: Candidates should avoid submitting multiple applications. If submitted due to unavoidable circumstances, only the application with the latest Registration Number, complete in all respects, will be considered. In case of multiple applications, fees paid against one Registration Number cannot be adjusted against another Registration Number. Candidates already in Government service must upload/obtain a No Objection Certificate (NOC) from their employer. Candidates must bear their own expenses for appearing in the selection test. The test centre, time, and duration will be communicated through the admit card. The online application portal will remain open from 10.07.2026 (11:00 A.M.) to 10.08.2026 (5:00 P.M.) for both posts.

rows.details: Junior Administrative Assistant, Senior Computer Assistant

content_sections.heading: Important Dates
content_sections.text: Candidates should note that the exact date, time, and venue of the written test will be mentioned in the admit card, which will be released at a later stage. It is advisable to keep checking the official website regularly for updates.

rows.event: Last Date to Submit Online Application
Written Test / Written Screening Test Date

content_sections.heading: Vacancy Details
content_sections.text: The Tripura High Court Recruitment 2026 drive covers two distinct posts with the following category-wise vacancy breakup: The number of posts to be filled is subject to increase or decrease as per requirement, and the selected panel of candidates will remain valid for one year from the date of its publication.

content_sections.heading: Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: Junior Administrative Assistant: Must be a Graduate from a recognized university. Must have a Certificate Course in Computer Word Processing or an equivalent qualification from a recognized university/institute. Must have a typing speed of 40 words per minute on a computer. Senior Computer Assistant: B.E./B.Tech in Computer Science/Information Technology, or MCA from any recognized university. As on 10.08.2026, candidates must be: Not below 18 years of age Not more than 40 years of age for candidates of other (general) categories Not more than 45 years of age for candidates belonging to Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes This age limit applies uniformly to both the Junior Administrative Assistant and Senior Computer Assistant posts.

rows.pay_structure: Entry pay of Rs. 21,400 per month in Pay Matrix Level-7 of TSCS (Revised Pay) (First Amendment) Rules, 2018, subject to revision by the Government from time to time
Regular scale Pay Band-2, Rs. 5,700-24,000 with Grade Pay of Rs. 2,800

content_sections.heading: Application Fee
content_sections.text: Fees must be paid online through Credit Card (Visa/Master/RuPay), Net Banking, Debit Card (Visa/Master/RuPay), Wallets, Cash Cards, or UPI only. No other mode of payment is accepted, and bank charges may apply. Fees once deposited will not be refunded or adjusted against any other recruitment in future. ST/SC candidates belonging to other states must apply as general category candidates and pay the fee applicable to the general category.

content_sections.heading: Selection Process
content_sections.text: Phase 1 - Written Screening Test (Objective Type, Duration: 2 hours, 100 Marks) Subjects: English, General Knowledge, Aptitude, Reasoning, Basic Computer Knowledge, etc. Minimum qualifying marks: 60% (relaxable by 10% for reserved category candidates). Shortlisting ratio for Phase 2: 1:3 (i.e., 3 candidates per advertised post, as far as practicable). Phase 2 - Type Test & Written Test (English) Minimum qualifying marks in Phase 2: 60%. Phase 1 - Written Test (Objective Type, Duration: 2 hours, 100 Marks) Subjects: General Knowledge, General English, Current Affairs, Aptitude, Knowledge of Computer Science and allied matters, etc. Minimum qualifying marks: 60% (relaxable by 10% for reserved category candidates). Shortlisting ratio for Phase 2: 1:3 (i.e., 3 candidates per advertised post, as far as practicable). Phase 2 - Viva Voce (Interview) & Skill Test Minimum qualifying marks in Phase 2: 70%. For both posts, if no suitable reserved-category candidate is available, the High Court will first attempt to fill the post from candidates of other reserved categories; if still unavailable, the post will be filled as per applicable rules by candidates outside the ST/SC category.

rows.component: Written Test (English) - Comprehension, Précis Writing, Paragraph Writing, Letter Writing, Translation (Bengali-English & vice versa), Writing Ability
Skill Test (Practical demonstration: knowledge of software and hardware)

content_sections.heading: Disqualification for Appointment
content_sections.text: A person is not eligible for appointment to the service if he/she: Is not a citizen of India Has been dismissed from service by the Government, Central Government, High Court, or any statutory/local authority Has been convicted of an offence involving moral turpitude Has more than one spouse living Directly or indirectly attempts to influence the recruiting authority by any means

content_sections.heading: Required Documents
content_sections.text: Educational qualification certificates and mark sheets Age proof / birth certificate Caste certificate (for SC/ST candidates), where applicable Computer certificate course document / typing speed certificate (for Junior Administrative Assistant) No Objection Certificate (NOC) from employer, for candidates already in Government service Valid photo identity proof Recent passport-size photograph and signature, as required in the online form Shortlisted candidates will be required to produce original mark sheets, certificates, and documents for scrutiny before appearing in the written test.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for Tripura High Court Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: The High Court will not entertain hard copy applications; submissions must be made only through the Online Application Portal.

content_sections.heading: Important Instructions
content_sections.text: Candidates should avoid submitting multiple applications. If submitted due to unavoidable circumstances, only the application with the latest Registration Number, complete in all respects, will be considered. In case of multiple applications, fees paid against one Registration Number cannot be adjusted against another Registration Number. Candidates already in Government service must upload/obtain a No Objection Certificate (NOC) from their employer. Candidates must bear their own expenses for appearing in the selection test. The test centre, time, and duration will be communicated through the admit card. The online application portal will remain open from 10.07.2026 (11:00 A.M.) to 10.08.2026 (5:00 P.M.) for both posts.

content_sections.text: Official Notification (Senior Computer Assistant - HC-03/2026): Click Here Official Notification (Junior Administrative Assistant - HC-02/2026): Click Here Official Website: Click Here

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for Tripura High Court Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The online application window closes on 10.08.2026 at 5:00 P.M. for both the Junior Administrative Assistant and Senior Computer Assistant posts.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are there in Tripura High Court Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There are a total of 17 vacancies — 14 for Junior Administrative Assistant and 3 for Senior Computer Assistant.

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for Tripura High Court Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must be between 18 and 40 years of age (45 years for SC/ST candidates) as on 10.08.2026, for both posts.

faq.question: Q4. What is the educational qualification required for Junior Administrative Assistant?
faq.answer: A graduate degree from a recognized university, along with a computer word processing certificate course and a typing speed of 40 words per minute.

faq.question: Q5. What is the educational qualification required for Senior Computer Assistant?
faq.answer: A B.E./B.Tech in Computer Science/Information Technology, or an MCA, from any recognized university.

faq.question: Q6. What is the application fee for Tripura High Court Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Rs. 500 for unreserved category candidates and Rs. 250 for SC/ST category candidates, for both posts.

faq.question: Q7. What is the selection process for these posts?
faq.answer: Both posts involve a two-phase selection process: a written test/screening test followed by a second phase (Viva Voce and Skill Test for Senior Computer Assistant; Type Test and Written English Test for Junior Administrative Assistant).

faq.question: Q8. Is there a provision for age relaxation?
faq.answer: Yes, candidates belonging to Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes are eligible for an upper age limit of 45 years, compared to 40 years for other categories.

faq.question: Q9. Where can I apply for Tripura High Court Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Applications must be submitted online only through the official High Court of Tripura website, thc.nic.in, under the "Recruitment" tab. Hard copy applications are not accepted.

faq.question: Q10. When will the written test date and admit card be released?
faq.answer: As per the official notification, the dates of the selection test and admit card download will be notified later on the official website.
